title: ReAct Prompt Structure
concept: Drafting the instruction system prompt that teaches a base model to execute the ReAct loop correctly.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: why do we force the model to write a Thought before it picks a tool?

options.text: To stop it from acting impulsively and make it plan first
To make the answer longer so it looks smarter
To slow the computer down on purpose

exercises.explain: Writing a Thought first forces the model to state its goal or hypothesis, so it reasons before grabbing a tool instead of acting on impulse.

cards.title: A Script for the Robot
cards.body: A base model doesn't automatically know the ReAct dance. You have to hand it a script telling it exactly how to Think, Act, and Observe.

cards.title: The ReAct System Prompt
cards.body: A ReAct system prompt is the instruction text that teaches the model the loop. It names the tools, shows the Thought/Action/Observation format, and tells the model to repeat until it can answer.

cards.title: Like a Recipe Card
cards.body: A recipe lists your ingredients (tools) and the exact steps in order. The ReAct prompt does the same: here are your tools, and here's the step pattern to follow every time.

cards.title: What Goes Inside
cards.body: A good prompt says: 'You have these tools: Search, Calculator. Always reply as Thought:, then Action:, then wait for Observation:. Repeat, then give a Final Answer.'

cards.title: Myth: The Model Just Knows
cards.body: Myth: a smart model figures out ReAct on its own. Reality: without a clear prompt showing the format, it often skips steps or invents fake tools.

cards.title: Vague vs. Clear Prompt
cards.body: The more structure your prompt gives, the more reliably the loop runs.

left.text: 'Use tools to answer.' Model guesses the format and messes up.

right.text: 'Reply as Thought / Action / Observation, tools: Search, Calc.' Model follows it.

exercises.prompt: Which item is MOST important to include in a ReAct system prompt?

options.text: The exact Thought / Action / Observation format the model must follow
A long history of the company
A joke to lighten the mood

exercises.explain: The prompt's main job is to teach the loop's format, so spelling out the Thought/Action/Observation pattern is essential.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A ReAct prompt should list which tools the model is allowed to use.
exercises.explain: Listing the available tools stops the model from inventing fake ones and keeps its Actions valid.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about the loop's structure.
exercises.sentence: The prompt tells the model to write a Thought, then an Action, then read the Observation, and repeat.
exercises.explain: The ReAct loop keeps cycling Thought, Action, Observation, so the prompt must tell the model to repeat until it can answer.

exercises.prompt: Which system prompt was AI-generated to teach a clean ReAct loop?

options.text: You may use Search and Calculator. Respond only in this cycle: Thought:, Action:, then pause for Observation:. Repeat until confident, then output Final Answer:.
just answer stuff and maybe use google idk
Be helpful and do your best with the question below.

exercises.explain: The correct one names the tools and spells out the exact Thought/Action/Observation cycle with a stopping rule, which is what a real ReAct prompt needs.

exercises.prompt: You're writing a ReAct system prompt. Which draft will make the base model run the loop most reliably?

options.text: 'Tools: Search, Calculator. For each step write Thought: (your reasoning), then Action: (tool + input). I will reply with Observation:. Repeat this cycle, then write Final Answer:.'
'Think hard and use whatever tools exist to solve it.'
'Answer the question. If needed, do some research first.'
'You are smart, so figure out the best steps yourself.'

exercises.explain: A strong ReAct prompt does three things: names the allowed tools, shows the exact Thought/Action/Observation format, and gives a repeat-then-stop rule. Vague prompts leave the model guessing, so it skips steps or invents tools. Spelling out the structure is what turns a base model into a reliable ReAct agent.
